subject: Veltrix Follow-Up — Apex Case Study, Demo Availability & Pricing Summary
body:
Hi Jamira, Tom, Chloe,

Really enjoyed our conversation today — thanks for being so open about the challenges with your current BambooHR + Sage + manual processes setup. The reconciliation burden Chloe described (a full day per week!) is exactly the kind of operational drag that a unified platform eliminates.

As promised:

1. Apex Engineering Case Study (attached)
620 employees, four countries, same performance calibration pain point you described. Their payroll errors dropped 84% and they ran their first data-backed promotion cycle in 14 years of company history. They're happy to take a reference call if you'd like to speak directly.

2. Pricing Summary
For 420 employees on Business plan (annual billing):
- Base HRIS: $11 PEPM × 420 × 12 = $55,440/year
- UK native payroll: Included in Business plan
- German payroll connector: $3 PEPM × 420 × 12 = $15,120/year
- Total: $70,560/year

For comparison, you're currently paying for BambooHR + Sage UK + German bureau fees + Chloe's reconciliation time. The consolidation is typically cost-neutral or positive on licence fees alone.

3. Demo Availability
I've got slots Tuesday 10am, Wednesday 2pm, or Thursday 11am GMT. I'll focus on the three things that resonated most:
- Calibration workflow (normalising manager ratings)
- Mandatory training automation (escalation chains)
- Veltrix Ask (natural language queries)

Let me know what works.

Best,
Ryan Mitchell
Account Executive | Veltrix
+44 20 7946 0958

subject: Re: Veltrix Follow-Up — Apex Case Study, Demo Availability & Pricing Summary
body:
Hi Ryan,

Thanks for this — the Apex case study is really compelling. I've shared it with our CEO and she's interested in the reference call. Can you arrange that?

Wednesday 2pm GMT works for the demo. I've added Marcus Reid (IT Manager) to this thread — he'll join for the security and integration questions.

A few things Marcus wanted me to ask ahead of the demo:

1. SSO: We use Azure AD. Is the integration straightforward or does it require custom config?

2. Data migration: We have about 6 years of employee records in BambooHR (roughly 2,800 records including leavers). What does migration look like?

3. German payroll connector: Our bureau is DATEV. Do you have a certified connector or is it a generic file export?

4. GDPR: We process employee health data (occupational health records, disability accommodations). How does Veltrix handle field-level encryption for sensitive PII?

Also, quick question from Chloe: Can we see the mandatory training escalation workflow in the demo? She wants to understand exactly what the manager notification looks like.

See you Wednesday.

Best,
Jamira

Jamira Okonkwo
HR Director | Hartwell Group

subject: Re: Veltrix Follow-Up — Apex Case Study, Demo Availability & Pricing Summary
body:
Hi Jamira, Marcus,

Great questions — let me address these directly:

1. Azure AD SSO
Native integration via SAML 2.0 or OIDC. Typical setup time is under 2 hours. SCIM 2.0 provisioning is also supported on Business plan, so user creation/deactivation in Azure AD automatically syncs to Veltrix.

2. Data Migration
2,800 records is well within our standard migration scope (up to 2,000 included free; we can handle 2,800 at no additional cost for a Business plan customer). We'll extract from BambooHR via their API, map fields to Veltrix schema, and do a validation pass before cutover. Historical records for leavers are preserved with full audit trail.

3. DATEV Connector
Yes — we have a certified DATEV connector specifically. It's not a generic file export. The connector pushes payroll inputs automatically each pay cycle and handles the DATEV-specific field mapping (Personalnummer, Lohnart codes, etc.).

4. GDPR & Field-Level Encryption
Veltrix applies field-level encryption (AES-256) to all sensitive PII fields — national ID, bank details, salary, and health-related data. This is an additional encryption layer on top of the at-rest encryption applied to all data. Access to health fields is restricted to users with explicit permissions (typically HR Director + Occupational Health roles). Full audit logging on every access event, exportable for SIEM.

We hold ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27701:2019 (Privacy Information Management) — the 27701 certification is specifically relevant for GDPR compliance and is something most HRIS vendors don't have.

Reference Call:
I've reached out to Apex — their CPO, Helen Park, is available Friday 3pm GMT for a 30-minute call if that works for your CEO.

Demo Wednesday:
Absolutely — I'll walk through the full mandatory training escalation workflow, including the manager notification email and the HRBP escalation trigger. Chloe will like this one.

See you then.

Best,
Ryan

subject: Veltrix Demo Recap + PoC Proposal
body:
Hi Jamira, Tom, Chloe, Marcus,

Thanks for a great session yesterday — really appreciated the engaged questions from everyone, especially Marcus's deep-dive on the SCIM provisioning flow.

Demo Recap:
- Calibration workflow: Showed the cross-department rating comparison view, outlier flagging, and the facilitated calibration session flow. Jamira — this directly addresses the "everyone gets a 5" problem from your last review cycle.
- Mandatory training automation: Built a sample compliance training track with 30/14/7-day reminders, manager escalation at 3 days, and HRBP escalation on deadline. Chloe — the notifications are fully templated and brandable.
- Veltrix Ask: Ran live queries including "how many people left London office last quarter" and "show me employees with incomplete mandatory training" — both returned results in under 2 seconds.

PoC Proposal:
Based on our discussion, I'd like to propose a 21-day Proof of Concept:

Phase 1 - Setup (Days 1–5): Tenant provisioning, Azure AD SSO, import 200 sample employee records
Phase 2 - Pilot (Days 6–15): 15 pilot users (HR team + 5 managers), configure UK payroll, build one performance review cycle
Phase 3 - Evaluation (Days 16–21): Structured feedback collection, ROI quantification, go/no-go decision

PoC is free. You get a dedicated Solutions Engineer (not me — someone more technical) for the duration.

If the PoC validates fit, we'd target full go-live by end of April — comfortably before your June review cycle.

Let me know if you'd like to proceed, and I'll send over the PoC scoping document.

Best,
Ryan
